articles.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

articles.title: UAP forum will let public hear from lawmakers, whistleblowers on disclosure 2026 | JUSTICE - IUSTITIA.BG 2025
articles.summary: The Disclosure Forum 2026, held Thursday in Washington, D.C., brought together nearly two dozen lawmakers, former intelligence officials, researchers, and whistleblowers to discuss UAP disclosure. Panels and roundtables, themed “Humanity at the Edge of Discovery,” covered security, defense, and policy implications and featured speakers such as Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep. Eric Burlison, and Rep. Tim Burchett. Disclosure Foundation board members Professor Avi Loeb and Mike Gold spoke, with Gold noting that a poll shows 89 % of Republicans and 88 % of Democrats want more UAP information and describing UAP as a national security issue. Loeb, recently appointed by the White House to lead the UAP Science Advisory Council, said the federal government must allocate resources to analyze existing data and gather clearer new data on UAP. The forum also highlighted a 2015 Department of Defense video labeled Gimbal that shows an unexplained object, underscoring the need for further investigation. Some advisory council members are skeptical of UAP claims, indicating that belief in UAP is not universal among participants.

articles.title: New science advisory council forms to help US government ‘resolve the UAP mystery’
articles.summary: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies. Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb has been appointed as an advisor and will lead the board’s UAP Science Advisory Council, a smaller working group focused on data-driven analysis. Loeb, who previously highlighted the interstellar object 3I/Atlas as a possible alien probe, says the program will have no budget, rely only on declassified data, and avoid public distraction. The board may investigate cases such as the 2023 glowing mothership orb near Cheyenne Mountain, and Loeb notes that 40% of observed phenomena cannot be explained by known U.S. or adversary technology. Loeb’s advisory team includes scientists Michael Shermer, Gary Nolan, Kevin Knuth, Matthew Szydagis, and retired Rear Admiral Gallaudet, who says the government is overdue to adopt a scientific perspective on UAP.

en.summary: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies.

de.title: Neue wissenschaftliche Beratungsgremien bilden sich, um der US-Regierung dabei zu helfen, „das UAP-Rätsel zu lösen“
de.summary: Die Trump-Administration hat ein neues UAP-Governance-Board eingerichtet, das sich mit nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen befassen soll, und hält am Dienstag seine erste Sitzung ab. Die Aufgabe des Gremiums besteht darin, die Richtlinie des Präsidenten zur UAP-Transparenz zu unterstützen, behördenübergreifende Prozesse zu integrieren und die Freigabe von UAP-Informationen zwischen Militär, Strafverfolgungsbehörden, Geheimdiensten und zivilen Behörden zu koordinieren.

Juni (Reuters) – Es wird erwartet, dass sich iranische und US-amerikanische technische Teams, die an der Umsetzung eines vorläufigen Friedensabkommens arbeiten, im Jahr treffen. Von Emma Farge GENF, 29. Juni (Reuters) – Ein ehemaliger politischer Gefangener aus Weißrussland äußerte am Montag Zweifel an einer Lockerung der Sanktionen gegen das osteuropäische Land als Gegenleistung für die Freilassung politischer Maßnahmen. Juni (Reuters) – Einwohner von Caracas erwachten am Montag zu einem Nachbeben, das ihre Häuser erschütterte, während Rettungsteams ihren vierten Tag fortsetzten. Der Oberste Gerichtshof entschied am Montag, dass Staaten Briefwahlzettel zählen können, die bis zum Wahltag abgegeben wurden, aber später eintreffen, und lehnte eine Anfechtung der Republikanischen Partei gegen einen ab Mississippi-Gesetz.

articles.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Box Office performance crashes like a UFO in Roswell
articles.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even. Because of the weak performance, Universal may pull the film from theatrical rotation if the trend continues, while the studio plans to accelerate online and digital releases. The movie stars Emily Blunt and is still currently playing in theaters, encouraging viewers to book tickets before it potentially closes.

en.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. de.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Die Vorstellung an den Kinokassen stürzt wie ein UFO in Roswell ab
de.summary: Steven Spielbergs Alien-Invasionsfilm „Disclosure Day“ startete am 12. Juni 2026 in den Kinos. Nach knapp zwei Wochen hat der Film weltweit 162,7 Millionen US-Dollar eingespielt, davon kamen nur 80,8 Millionen US-Dollar aus Nordamerika. Die Einnahmen des Films gingen am zweiten Wochenende um 62 % zurück, und die Produktionskosten von 115 Millionen US-Dollar bedeuten, dass an den Kinokassen etwa 280 Millionen US-Dollar nötig wären, um die Gewinnschwelle zu erreichen.

articles.title: Newly released UFO files allege China and Russia retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse-engineer them
articles.summary: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. Flowers cited whistleblower David Grusch, who warned that the U.S. is aware of Russian and Chinese surveillance of its UAP programs and that a global race to reverse‑engineer the objects has extreme national‑security implications. The UAP Disclosure Foundation announced a forum in Washington, D.C., on June 25 to discuss the national‑security, technological, and religious implications of UAP disclosure. A CIA cable from July 2, 2008 placed U.S. assets in Zimbabwe on high alert after a UFO hovered over Harare airport, with beams and rotating lights, sparking debate over whether the craft was a foreign reconnaissance device or an extraterrestrial object.

en.summary: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. de.title: In neu veröffentlichten UFO-Dateien wird behauptet, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs zurückgeholt und versucht hätten, sie zurückzuentwickeln
de.summary: Neu veröffentlichte UFO-Dateien, die am 12. Juni veröffentlicht wurden, behaupten, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs geborgen und versucht haben, sie zurückzuentwickeln. Jordan Flowers, Geschäftsführer der UAP Disclosure Foundation, sagte, die Akten zeigten, dass ausländische Gegner an Forschungsarbeiten beteiligt seien, die die nationale Sicherheit der USA gefährden könnten. Flowers zitierte den Whistleblower David Grusch, der warnte, dass die USA sich der Überwachung ihrer UAP-Programme durch Russland und China bewusst seien und dass ein weltweiter Wettlauf um die Rückentwicklung der Objekte extreme Auswirkungen auf die nationale Sicherheit habe.

articles.title: Pentagon Report Documents Giant Potato-Shaped UFO Over Colorado Springs
articles.summary: In February 2022, five U.S. Army service members near Cheyenne, Colorado, observed a translucent, milky, potato‑shaped object that shimmered and had irregular, fish‑scale‑like panels that shifted in slow waves before vanishing after about two minutes; the witness reported it cloaked quickly and cast no shadow. A Department of Defense report attributes the sighting to possible backscattering of sunlight from the sun over the mountain, offering a potential natural explanation. Wyoming attorney Rich Barrett, who saw a massive noiseless device with his father in 1991, says he does not expect answers for either that event or the 2022 Army sighting, though he notes increased transparency under the Trump administration. New Jersey Army National Guard Colonel David Melendez considers the object could be an experimental U.S. craft but remains open to extraterrestrial possibilities, noting that the U.S. military may withhold details on a need‑to‑know basis and that UAP videos are often grainy. Wyoming officials, including Adjutant General Greg Porter and lawmakers, have reported no UAP incidents over state airspace, though some sightings near federal facilities have been noted; a bill to allow the governor to activate the National Guard against drone incursions was vetoed and later overridden. Eight Wyoming sheriffs confirmed reports of strange drone‑like sightings in January 2025, with the Sweetwater County Sheriff describing them as the "new normal" and stating that the objects have not caused harm but would be acted upon if they posed imminent danger.

en.title: Pentagon Report Documents Giant Potato-Shaped UFO Over Colorado Springs
en.summary: In February 2022, five U.S. Army service members near Cheyenne, Colorado, observed a translucent, milky, potato‑shaped object that shimmered and had irregular, fish‑scale‑like panels that shifted in slow waves before vanishing after about two minutes; the witness reported it cloaked quickly and cast no shadow. A Department of Defense report attributes the sighting to possible backscattering of sunlight from the sun over the mountain, offering a potential natural explanation.

de.title: Pentagon-Bericht dokumentiert riesiges kartoffelförmiges UFO über Colorado Springs
de.summary: Im Februar 2022 beobachteten fünf Angehörige der US-Armee in der Nähe von Cheyenne, Colorado, ein durchscheinendes, milchiges, kartoffelförmiges Objekt, das schimmerte und unregelmäßige, fischschuppenartige Felder aufwies, die sich in langsamen Wellen bewegten, bevor sie nach etwa zwei Minuten verschwanden; Der Zeuge berichtete, dass es sich schnell verhüllte und keinen Schatten warf. Ein Bericht des Verteidigungsministeriums führt die Sichtung auf eine mögliche Rückstreuung des Sonnenlichts von der Sonne über dem Berg zurück und bietet damit eine mögliche natürliche Erklärung.

summaries.file-release-department-of-war-publishes: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.
summaries.sighting-uap-forum-will-let-public-hear-: The Disclosure Forum 2026, held Thursday in Washington, D.C., brought together nearly two dozen lawmakers, former intelligence officials, researchers, and whistleblowers to discuss UAP disclosure. Panels and roundtables, themed “Humanity at the Edge of Discovery,” covered security, defense, and policy implications and featured speakers such as Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep. Eric Burlison, and Rep. Tim Burchett. Disclosure Foundation board members Professor Avi Loeb and Mike Gold spoke, with Gold noting that a poll shows 89 % of Republicans and 88 % of Democrats want more UAP information and describing UAP as a national security issue. Loeb, recently appointed by the White House to lead the UAP Science Advisory Council, said the federal government must allocate resources to analyze existing data and gather clearer new data on UAP. The forum also highlighted a 2015 Department of Defense video labeled Gimbal that shows an unexplained object, underscoring the need for further investigation. Some advisory council members are skeptical of UAP claims, indicating that belief in UAP is not universal among participants.
summaries.program-new-science-advisory-council-for: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies. Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb has been appointed as an advisor and will lead the board’s UAP Science Advisory Council, a smaller working group focused on data-driven analysis. Loeb, who previously highlighted the interstellar object 3I/Atlas as a possible alien probe, says the program will have no budget, rely only on declassified data, and avoid public distraction. The board may investigate cases such as the 2023 glowing mothership orb near Cheyenne Mountain, and Loeb notes that 40% of observed phenomena cannot be explained by known U.S. or adversary technology. Loeb’s advisory team includes scientists Michael Shermer, Gary Nolan, Kevin Knuth, Matthew Szydagis, and retired Rear Admiral Gallaudet, who says the government is overdue to adopt a scientific perspective on UAP.
summaries.film-disclosure-day-2026-box-office-perf: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even. Because of the weak performance, Universal may pull the film from theatrical rotation if the trend continues, while the studio plans to accelerate online and digital releases. The movie stars Emily Blunt and is still currently playing in theaters, encouraging viewers to book tickets before it potentially closes.
summaries.file-release-newly-released-ufo-files-al: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. Flowers cited whistleblower David Grusch, who warned that the U.S. is aware of Russian and Chinese surveillance of its UAP programs and that a global race to reverse‑engineer the objects has extreme national‑security implications. The UAP Disclosure Foundation announced a forum in Washington, D.C., on June 25 to discuss the national‑security, technological, and religious implications of UAP disclosure. A CIA cable from July 2, 2008 placed U.S. assets in Zimbabwe on high alert after a UFO hovered over Harare airport, with beams and rotating lights, sparking debate over whether the craft was a foreign reconnaissance device or an extraterrestrial object.
summaries.file-release-pentagon-report-documents-g: In February 2022, five U.S. Army service members near Cheyenne, Colorado, observed a translucent, milky, potato‑shaped object that shimmered and had irregular, fish‑scale‑like panels that shifted in slow waves before vanishing after about two minutes; the witness reported it cloaked quickly and cast no shadow. A Department of Defense report attributes the sighting to possible backscattering of sunlight from the sun over the mountain, offering a potential natural explanation. Wyoming attorney Rich Barrett, who saw a massive noiseless device with his father in 1991, says he does not expect answers for either that event or the 2022 Army sighting, though he notes increased transparency under the Trump administration. New Jersey Army National Guard Colonel David Melendez considers the object could be an experimental U.S. craft but remains open to extraterrestrial possibilities, noting that the U.S. military may withhold details on a need‑to‑know basis and that UAP videos are often grainy. Wyoming officials, including Adjutant General Greg Porter and lawmakers, have reported no UAP incidents over state airspace, though some sightings near federal facilities have been noted; a bill to allow the governor to activate the National Guard against drone incursions was vetoed and later overridden. Eight Wyoming sheriffs confirmed reports of strange drone‑like sightings in January 2025, with the Sweetwater County Sheriff describing them as the "new normal" and stating that the objects have not caused harm but would be acted upon if they posed imminent danger.
summaries.new-uap-panel-to-advise-feds-a-completel: New UAP panel to advise feds: ‘A completely different mindset’ | Elizabeth Vargas Reports Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb tells "Elizabeth Vargas Reports" there are reasons to be optimistic about the new UAP Advisory Council he'll lead under the Trump administration: "We are trying to figure it out together, in cooperation, and I hope that all the suspicion against government hiding things will be forgotten once we get to the bottom of this. "#UFO #Trump By Andrew Mills, Parisa Hafezi and Humeyra Pamuk DUBAI/WASHINGTON, June 29 (Reuters) - Iranian and U. technical teams working on the implementation of an interim peace deal are expected to meet in By Emma Farge GENEVA, June 29 (Reuters) - An ex-political prisoner from Belarus on Monday raised doubts about easing sanctions on the Eastern European country in return for releasing political By Vivian Sequera and Aida Pelaez-Fernandez CARACAS, June 29 (Reuters) - Residents of Caracas woke up on Monday to an aftershock that rocked their houses, while rescue teams continued their fourth day The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.

The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

de.summary: Das Disclosure Forum 2026, das am Donnerstag in Washington, D.C. stattfand, brachte fast zwei Dutzend Gesetzgeber, ehemalige Geheimdienstmitarbeiter, Forscher und Whistleblower zusammen, um über die Offenlegung von UAP zu diskutieren. Redner waren unter anderem Senatorin Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep.

en.title: New science advisory council forms to help US government ‘resolve the UAP mystery’
en.summary: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies.

de.title: Neue wissenschaftliche Beratungsgremien bilden sich, um der US-Regierung dabei zu helfen, „das UAP-Rätsel zu lösen“
de.summary: Die Trump-Administration hat ein neues UAP-Governance-Board eingerichtet, das sich mit nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen befassen soll, und hält am Dienstag seine erste Sitzung ab. Die Aufgabe des Gremiums besteht darin, die Richtlinie des Präsidenten zur UAP-Transparenz zu unterstützen, behördenübergreifende Prozesse zu integrieren und die Freigabe von UAP-Informationen zwischen Militär, Strafverfolgungsbehörden, Geheimdiensten und zivilen Behörden zu koordinieren.

en.summary: New UAP panel to advise feds: ‘A completely different mindset’ | Elizabeth Vargas Reports Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb tells "Elizabeth Vargas Reports" there are reasons to be optimistic about the new UAP Advisory Council he'll lead under the Trump administration: "We are trying to figure it out together, in cooperation, and I hope that all the suspicion against government hiding things will be forgotten once we get to the bottom of this. "#UFO #Trump By Andrew Mills, Parisa Hafezi and Humeyra Pamuk DUBAI/WASHINGTON, June 29 (Reuters) - Iranian and U. technical teams working on the implementation of an interim peace deal are expected to meet in By Emma Farge GENEVA, June 29 (Reuters) - An ex-political prisoner from Belarus on Monday raised doubts about easing sanctions on the Eastern European country in return for releasing political By Vivian Sequera and Aida Pelaez-Fernandez CARACAS, June 29 (Reuters) - Residents of Caracas woke up on Monday to an aftershock that rocked their houses, while rescue teams continued their fourth day The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.

de.title: Neues UAP-Gremium berät die Regierung: „Eine völlig andere Denkweise“ | Elizabeth Vargas Berichte
de.summary: Neues UAP-Gremium berät die Regierung: „Eine völlig andere Denkweise“ | Elizabeth Vargas Reports Der Harvard-Astrophysiker Avi Loeb sagt gegenüber „Elizabeth Vargas Reports“, dass es Gründe gibt, optimistisch zu sein, was den neuen UAP-Beirat angeht, den er unter der Trump-Administration leiten wird: „Wir versuchen, es gemeinsam und in Zusammenarbeit herauszufinden, und ich hoffe, dass der ganze Verdacht gegen die Regierung, Dinge zu verbergen, vergessen wird, wenn wir der Sache auf den Grund gehen.“ #UFO #Trump Von Andrew Mills, Parisa Hafezi und Humeyra Pamuk DUBAI/WASHINGTON, 29. Juni (Reuters) – Es wird erwartet, dass sich iranische und US-amerikanische technische Teams, die an der Umsetzung eines vorläufigen Friedensabkommens arbeiten, im Jahr treffen. Von Emma Farge GENF, 29. Juni (Reuters) – Ein ehemaliger politischer Gefangener aus Weißrussland äußerte am Montag Zweifel an einer Lockerung der Sanktionen gegen das osteuropäische Land als Gegenleistung für die Freilassung politischer Maßnahmen. Von Vivian Sequera und Aida Pelaez-Fernandez CARACAS, 29. Juni (Reuters) – Einwohner von Caracas erwachten am Montag zu einem Nachbeben, das ihre Häuser erschütterte, während Rettungsteams ihren vierten Tag fortsetzten. Der Oberste Gerichtshof entschied am Montag, dass Staaten Briefwahlzettel zählen können, die bis zum Wahltag abgegeben wurden, aber später eintreffen, und lehnte eine Anfechtung der Republikanischen Partei gegen einen ab Mississippi-Gesetz.

en.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Box Office performance crashes like a UFO in Roswell
en.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even.

de.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Die Vorstellung an den Kinokassen stürzt wie ein UFO in Roswell ab
de.summary: Steven Spielbergs Alien-Invasionsfilm „Disclosure Day“ startete am 12. Juni 2026 in den Kinos. Nach knapp zwei Wochen hat der Film weltweit 162,7 Millionen US-Dollar eingespielt, davon kamen nur 80,8 Millionen US-Dollar aus Nordamerika. Die Einnahmen des Films gingen am zweiten Wochenende um 62 % zurück, und die Produktionskosten von 115 Millionen US-Dollar bedeuten, dass an den Kinokassen etwa 280 Millionen US-Dollar nötig wären, um die Gewinnschwelle zu erreichen.

en.title: Newly released UFO files allege China and Russia retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse-engineer them
en.summary: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. Flowers cited whistleblower David Grusch, who warned that the U.S. is aware of Russian and Chinese surveillance of its UAP programs and that a global race to reverse‑engineer the objects has extreme national‑security implications.

de.title: In neu veröffentlichten UFO-Dateien wird behauptet, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs zurückgeholt und versucht hätten, sie zurückzuentwickeln
de.summary: Neu veröffentlichte UFO-Dateien, die am 12. Juni veröffentlicht wurden, behaupten, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs geborgen und versucht haben, sie zurückzuentwickeln. Jordan Flowers, Geschäftsführer der UAP Disclosure Foundation, sagte, die Akten zeigten, dass ausländische Gegner an Forschungsarbeiten beteiligt seien, die die nationale Sicherheit der USA gefährden könnten. Flowers zitierte den Whistleblower David Grusch, der warnte, dass die USA sich der Überwachung ihrer UAP-Programme durch Russland und China bewusst seien und dass ein weltweiter Wettlauf um die Rückentwicklung der Objekte extreme Auswirkungen auf die nationale Sicherheit habe.
